# ID-43: OQ-escalation channel — durable worker↔orchestrator open-question protocol

Implement the durable Open-Question (OQ) escalation/decision channel specified in docs/specs/oq-escalation/PRODUCT.md (33 invariants OQ-INV-1..33): a cmux sub-worker running workflow-orchestration mid-Task emits a self-contained immutable OQ record (oq_id, worker_id, urgency, blocking flag, context_ref) to its parent orchestrator and receives an addressed decision back. Covers blocking vs non-blocking semantics, per-worker FIFO ordering, atomic/durable/append-only idempotent emission, cancellation, at-least-once decision delivery with a 10s default latency bound, an awaiting-decision worker state, and crash/restart safety for both worker and parent.

## Provenance (kh-S260)

Re-instated as a tracked Task during S260 housekeeping. The PRODUCT.md spec shipped at S62 (commit a47066c4) but was NEVER tracked by any Task/Subtask — a full git-history walk (S260 Agent-D audit) confirmed zero task/subtask ever titled escalation/oq. It survived only as prose mentions inside ID-27 (done; 'mirrors OQ-escalation channel shape' — the --brief flag dogfooded the pattern manually). The spec is therefore orphaned at the TECH + implementation level: PRODUCT done, no TECH.md, 3 open PRODUCT-invariant questions unratified. ID-27 (session-driver-cmux) is its closest relative.

## Acceptance criteria

- TECH.md authored resolving transport (JSONL / SQLite / named-pipe / cmux send) + OQ-Q1 (received ack), OQ-Q2 (max awaiting-decision duration), OQ-Q3 (non-blocking OQ cap).
- Channel implements all 33 PRODUCT invariants with tests; a worker can emit a blocking OQ, enter awaiting-decision, and resume on an addressed decision within the latency bound.
- Crash/restart safety verified for both worker and parent (durable append-only store survives restart; idempotent re-emission by stable oq_id).

## Dependencies

None hard. Sibling to ID-27 (session-driver-cmux) + the cmux dogfooding line; orchestration/dev-workflow theme.
